tasks.title: Email Template Branding v1
tasks.description: Implement MJML email templates with brand token substitution and v1 From/Reply-To headers
tasks.details: MJML templates src/email/templates/*.mjml (invite, password-reset, billing, audit). Token replace {{brand.name}}, {{brand.logo_url}}, {{brand.primary_color}}. From: "{brand.name} via Conduit" <noreply@mail.conduit.wyre.io>, Reply-To: brand.support_email. Render MJML→HTML+text via mjml npm. SPF/DKIM aligned.
tasks.testStrategy: Snapshot test rendered HTML per brand × template, email client render test (Litmus/EmailOnAcid), no Wyre strings, correct From/Reply-To headers

subtasks.title: Create MJML Templates with Brand Tokens
subtasks.description: Develop MJML email templates for invite, password-reset, billing, and audit types in src/email/templates/*.mjml with placeholders for brand tokens.
subtasks.details: Include {{brand.name}}, {{brand.logo_url}}, {{brand.primary_color}} tokens in appropriate sections like headers, logos, and color styling. Use MJML components (mj-section, mj-column, mj-image, mj-text) for responsive design.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate MJML syntax with mjml validate command, preview rendered HTML in MJML app for each template.

subtasks.title: Implement Brand Token Substitution Function
subtasks.description: Create a function to replace brand tokens in MJML templates using resolved brand data from req.brand.
subtasks.details: Build tokenReplace(template, brand) that substitutes {{brand.name}}, {{brand.logo_url}}, {{brand.primary_color}} with actual values. Handle missing tokens gracefully and escape special characters for MJML/HTML safety.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for token replacement with sample brand data, edge cases (missing brand fields, special chars), snapshot comparison before/after substitution.

subtasks.title: Build MJML to HTML/Text Rendering Pipeline
subtasks.description: Integrate mjml npm package to compile substituted MJML templates into HTML and plain text versions.
subtasks.details: Create renderEmail(templatePath, brand) function that: 1) loads MJML, 2) substitutes tokens, 3) compiles via mjml2html(), 4) generates plain text version. Cache compiled results per brand+template.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests rendering full pipeline per template x brand, validate HTML structure, test plain text extraction accuracy.

subtasks.title: Configure Dynamic From/Reply-To Headers
subtasks.description: Implement brand-specific From and Reply-To headers for email sending service integration.
subtasks.details: Format From: '{brand.name} via Conduit' <noreply@mail.conduit.wyre.io>, Reply-To: brand.support_email. Create getEmailHeaders(brand) function returning standardized header object. Ensure fallback to Wyre defaults.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests generating headers for customer/MSP/Wyre brands, validate email address format compliance, test fallback logic.

subtasks.title: Add Email Testing and SPF/DKIM Validation
subtasks.description: Implement comprehensive testing suite and verify SPF/DKIM alignment for branded emails.
subtasks.details: Create snapshot tests for rendered HTML per brand×template combo. Integrate Litmus/EmailOnAcid testing workflow. Validate no Wyre strings leak into customer brands. Test From/Reply-To headers in mock email service.
subtasks.testStrategy: Snapshot tests (100% coverage), Litmus/EmailOnAcid render tests across 30+ clients, regex scans for Wyre strings, header validation tests.

tasks.title: Wyre Operator Preview Mode
tasks.description: Implement brand preview cookie + banner + audit logging for operators
tasks.details: POST /admin/brand-preview → set HttpOnly SameSite=Strict cookie brand_preview={brand_id} (30min TTL). Middleware checks cookie → overrides resolution. Persistent banner "Previewing as {brand.name} — [Exit]". Audit brand_preview_audit table on start/end. admin.conduit.wyre.io hard-pins wyreDefault ignoring cookie.
tasks.testStrategy: E2E: operator→preview→customer view with banner, exit→wyreDefault, audit row written, cookie security headers correct, admin host overrides

subtasks.title: Create POST /admin/brand-preview Endpoint
subtasks.description: Implement the POST endpoint to set the brand preview cookie with specified security attributes and TTL.
subtasks.details: In the admin routes file, create POST /admin/brand-preview handler that validates brand_id exists, sets HttpOnly SameSite=Strict cookie 'brand_preview={brand_id}' with 30min TTL, returns success response. Ensure auth middleware protects endpoint.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: valid brand_id sets cookie correctly, invalid brand_id returns 404, unauthorized access returns 401. Verify cookie attributes via response headers.

subtasks.title: Implement Brand Preview Middleware
subtasks.description: Create middleware that reads brand_preview cookie and overrides brand resolution logic.
subtasks.details: In src/brand/preview-middleware.ts, parse brand_preview cookie, validate brand_id exists in DB, override req.brand resolution to use preview brand instead of host/org resolution. Skip for admin.conduit.wyre.io host.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: cookie present→override resolution, invalid brand_id→ignore cookie, admin host→ignore cookie and use wyreDefault, no cookie→normal resolution.

subtasks.title: Build Persistent Preview Banner UI
subtasks.description: Create React component for persistent top banner showing preview brand name with Exit button.
subtasks.details: In frontend components, create BrandPreviewBanner that reads req.brand.isPreview flag, displays 'Previewing as {brand.name} — [Exit]' where Exit clears cookie via POST /admin/brand-preview-exit or similar. Style as persistent yellow bar.
subtasks.testStrategy: Component tests: renders correct brand name, Exit button clears cookie and reloads, persists across page navigation, responsive design.

subtasks.title: Setup brand_preview_audit Table Schema
subtasks.description: Create database table and migration for audit logging of preview mode start/end events.
subtasks.details: Add to existing brand migration or create new: brand_preview_audit table with columns id, operator_user_id, brand_id, action(start/end), ip_address, user_agent, created_at. Include indexes on operator_user_id, brand_id, created_at.
subtasks.testStrategy: Migration test: table created with correct schema/constraints, indexes present. Insert test rows validate constraints.

subtasks.title: Implement Audit Logging and Admin Host Override
subtasks.description: Add audit log entries on preview start/end and enforce wyreDefault for admin host.
subtasks.details: In preview middleware/endpoint: log INSERT to brand_preview_audit on cookie set/clear with operator context, timestamp, IP/UA. In host resolver: hard-pin admin.conduit.wyre.io → wyreDefault regardless of cookie. Add Exit POST endpoint.
subtasks.testStrategy: E2E: operator sets preview→audit row written, views customer site→banner+preview brand, exits→audit end row+wyreDefault, admin host always wyreDefault, cookie security headers validated.

tasks.title: Env-Var to DB Migration Script
tasks.description: Create idempotent scripts/migrate-brand-env-to-db.ts importing BRAND_* + customers.ts
tasks.details: Read process.env BRAND_* → insert/update brand_profiles is_wyre_default=true. Parse src/brand/customers.ts hardcoded registry → import by org slug. Dry-run mode (--dry-run). Idempotent (UPSERT on version check). Docs in README. Handles missing env vars gracefully.
tasks.testStrategy: Run on test env with BRAND_* set → correct DB rows, run twice→no duplicates, missing env→skips gracefully, customers.ts import verified

subtasks.title: Create TypeScript migration script skeleton
subtasks.description: Initialize migrate-brand-env-to-db.ts with CLI argument parsing, DB connection setup, and basic structure for env var reading and customers.ts parsing.
subtasks.details: Use commander.js or yargs for --dry-run flag. Establish Prisma or pg client connection. Add graceful exit on missing DB_URL. Structure with async main() function handling all phases sequentially.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify script runs without errors, parses --dry-run correctly, connects to test DB, logs missing env vars appropriately

subtasks.title: Implement BRAND_* env vars to brand_profiles UPSERT
subtasks.description: Read all process.env BRAND_* variables and perform idempotent UPSERT into brand_profiles table with is_wyre_default=true.
subtasks.details: Filter env vars starting with 'BRAND_'. Map to brand_profiles columns (name, primary_color, etc.). Use version check for idempotency: UPDATE if version matches, INSERT otherwise. Set is_wyre_default=true. Skip if no BRAND_* vars found.
subtasks.testStrategy: Set test BRAND_* env vars → verify single row created/updated, run twice → no duplicates, version increment works, missing vars → logs skip message

subtasks.title: Parse customers.ts and import org slugs to brand_profiles
subtasks.description: Parse src/brand/customers.ts hardcoded registry and create/update brand_profiles entries by org slug.
subtasks.details: Use tsx or esbuild to execute/parse customers.ts safely. Extract org slugs from registry export. For each slug, UPSERT brand_profiles with org_slug, default values, is_wyre_default=false. Use same version-based idempotency logic.
subtasks.testStrategy: Mock customers.ts with 3 orgs → verify 3 rows created, re-run → no duplicates, malformed customers.ts → graceful error handling

subtasks.title: Add dry-run mode and full idempotency safeguards
subtasks.description: Implement --dry-run flag that logs SQL without executing, plus comprehensive idempotency checks using version field.
subtasks.details: In dry-run: console.log all generated UPSERT queries instead of execute. Always check existing version before any UPDATE/INSERT. Transaction wrapper for atomicity. Validate hex colors match ^#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}$ before processing.
subtasks.testStrategy: --dry-run → verify SQL logged correctly no DB changes, normal run → DB updated, twice → idempotent no changes second run

subtasks.title: Add documentation and error handling
subtasks.description: Document script usage in README.md and implement graceful handling for all error cases including missing env vars.
subtasks.details: README section: usage, env vars expected, --dry-run behavior, idempotency guarantee, example output. Handle missing env vars with warnings, DB connection failures with retry, customers.ts parse errors with fallback, constraint violations with detailed logs.
subtasks.testStrategy: Missing env vars → warning logs + clean exit(0), DB down → retry then exit(1), invalid hex color → skip with log, full run → README instructions verifiable

tasks.title: Feature Flag and Backwards Compatibility
tasks.description: Wire FEATURE_DB_BRANDING flag with env-var fallback path
tasks.details: src/brand/index.ts: if !FEATURE_DB_BRANDING || no DB brand → fallback to existing env-var loader + customers.ts registry. DB wins when flag=true. Clean rollback: flag=false restores old behavior exactly. Update all call sites to use new resolveBrand().
tasks.testStrategy: Integration test: flag=false→existing brand.test.ts unchanged, flag=true→DB resolution, mixed mode (some orgs DB, some env), no regressions

subtasks.title: Implement Feature Flag Logic in src/brand/index.ts
subtasks.description: Add conditional logic to check FEATURE_DB_BRANDING flag and fallback to env-var loader or customers.ts when flag is false or no DB brand exists.
subtasks.details: In src/brand/index.ts, create if statement: if (!FEATURE_DB_BRANDING || !dbBrand) { use existing env-var loader + customers.ts registry }. Ensure DB brand takes precedence when flag=true. Use process.env for flag with boolean coercion.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test flag=false → env-var/customers.ts path, flag=true → DB path, no DB brand → fallback correctly

subtasks.title: Create Unified resolveBrand() Function
subtasks.description: Develop new resolveBrand() function that encapsulates the feature flag logic and resolution hierarchy.
subtasks.details: Export resolveBrand(req) from src/brand/index.ts that internally calls resolver core or fallback based on flag. Include version and domain computation. Handle edge cases like no brand found.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test resolveBrand() covers all paths: DB hit, fallback, wyre-default, ensures clean separation

subtasks.title: Identify and Update All Brand Resolution Call Sites
subtasks.description: Locate existing code calling old brand loaders and replace with new resolveBrand() calls.
subtasks.details: Grep codebase for env-var loader and customers.ts usage in brand resolution contexts. Replace with resolveBrand(req). Ensure req context provides org_id/host correctly.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: all call sites resolve correctly under flag=false (no behavior change)

subtasks.title: Verify Backwards Compatibility and Rollback Mechanism
subtasks.description: Test that flag=false exactly restores old behavior and document rollback procedure.
subtasks.details: Confirm env-var loader + customers.ts unchanged. Add README section: 'Rollback: set FEATURE_DB_BRANDING=false'. Ensure no side effects or data loss on toggle.
subtasks.testStrategy: Full regression: flag=false → existing brand.test.ts passes identically, mixed orgs work

subtasks.title: Add Integration Tests for Feature Flag Scenarios
subtasks.description: Implement comprehensive tests covering flag states, mixed resolution modes, and no regressions.
subtasks.details: Extend brand.test.ts: flag=false (unchanged), flag=true (DB wins), mixed (some orgs DB, some env-var), cache invalidation compatibility. Mock env vars and DB.
subtasks.testStrategy: CI tests pass 100%: flag toggle, mixed resolution, performance ≤ existing P95, zero regressions

tasks.title: Observability, Metrics, and Alerts
tasks.description: Implement all required Prometheus metrics, audit events, and alerting rules
tasks.details: OTel histograms/gauge/counters: conduit.brand.resolve_ms{cache_layer}, cache_hit_ratio, custom_domain.status, brand.asset_upload_bytes. Audit events: brand.profile.{created,updated}, domain.{added,verified,failed}, preview.{started,ended}. Alerts: cert expiry <14d, DNS backlog >50, cache hit <0.9.
tasks.testStrategy: Metrics emit on brand resolve/upload, Grafana dashboard verifies, alerts fire on threshold breach, audit events searchable

subtasks.title: Implement Brand Resolve Metrics
subtasks.description: Add OTel histogram for conduit_brand_resolve_ms with cache_layer label and gauge for cache_hit_ratio
subtasks.details: In src/brand/resolver.ts, instrument resolveBrand() with Histogram.record() for duration_ms {cache_layer='l1|l2|db'} and Gauge.set(1|0) for cache hit ratio. Follow Prometheus naming: base units, single prefix, low cardinality labels[1][3][5]
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests verify metrics emit on resolve paths; curl /metrics shows correct labels/values; Grafana query confirms histogram buckets

subtasks.title: Implement Domain and Asset Metrics
subtasks.description: Add gauge for custom_domain_status and counter for brand_asset_upload_bytes
subtasks.details: In host-resolver.ts and asset upload handlers: Gauge.set(0|1|2) for custom_domain_status{status='active|pending|failed'} and Counter.add(bytes) for brand_asset_upload_bytes{org_id}. Use consistent conduit_ prefix and limit label cardinality[3][4]
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests: resolve custom domain emits status gauge; upload 1MB asset increments counter; verify no high-cardinality labels in /metrics

subtasks.title: Implement Audit Events Logging
subtasks.description: Create audit event emitter for brand.profile/domain/preview events using structured logging
subtasks.details: In brand service handlers: emit JSON logs for brand_profile_{created,updated}, domain_{added,verified,failed}, preview_{started,ended} with org_id, user_id, timestamp, metadata. Use structured logger compatible with audit search (ELK/OpenSearch)[1]
subtasks.testStrategy: Trigger events via API; verify structured logs appear in audit index; search/filter by org_id/event_type returns correct records

subtasks.title: Define Prometheus Alerting Rules
subtasks.description: Create alerting rules YAML for cert_expiry<14d, DNS_backlog>50, cache_hit_ratio<0.9
subtasks.details: In prometheus/rules/brand_alerts.yaml: for expr=ssl_certificate_expiry_seconds<14*24*3600, dns_queue_length>50, rate(cache_misses[5m])/rate(cache_total[5m])>0.1. Set for=5m, labels={severity='critical|warning'}. Test with promtool[2]
subtasks.testStrategy: promtool check rules verifies syntax; inject test values trigger alerts in Prometheus UI; Grafana alert dashboard shows firing state

subtasks.title: Verification and Dashboard Setup
subtasks.description: Create Grafana dashboard and end-to-end verification of metrics/audits/alerts
subtasks.details: Build Grafana JSON dashboard with panels for resolve latency P95, cache hit ratio, domain status heatmap, audit event rate, alert status table. Add /metrics healthcheck endpoint. Document verification checklist[1][2]
subtasks.testStrategy: Load test generates traffic; verify all metrics populate correctly; breach thresholds trigger alerts; audit logs searchable; dashboard matches expected values

tasks.title: Migration: onboarding_progress table + orgs.kind/trial_ends_at columns
tasks.description: Create migration 002_onboarding_progress.sql to add onboarding_progress table and columns orgs.kind, orgs.review_required, orgs.trial_ends_at as specified in PRD §7.5 and §4.8.
tasks.details:
[CROSS-TAG BLOCKER] onboarding_progress + orgs.kind columns must follow reseller-tenancy #1-3 to avoid schema conflicts.

Run `npm run db:migrate:generate 002_onboarding_progress`; implement exact SQL schema from PRD §7.5 including indexes. Add CHECK constraint on funnel column. Update orgs table with ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN kind TEXT CHECK (kind IN ('reseller','customer')), review_required BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, trial_ends_at TIMESTAMPTZ. Use gen_random_uuid() for id.
tasks.testStrategy: Run migration forward/backward. Verify table schema with `\d onboarding_progress` and `\d orgs`. Insert test row and confirm UNIQUE constraint fires on duplicate (user_id, org_id, funnel). Check indexes exist.

subtasks.title: Generate onboarding_progress migration file
subtasks.description: Run the npm command to scaffold the SQL migration file 002_onboarding_progress.sql
subtasks.details: Execute `npm run db:migrate:generate 002_onboarding_progress` to create the base migration file with up/down functions in the migrations directory.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify the file is created with correct timestamp prefix and empty up/down functions.

subtasks.title: Implement onboarding_progress table schema
subtasks.description: Add CREATE TABLE statement for onboarding_progress with exact schema from PRD §7.5 including id with gen_random_uuid(), user_id, org_id, funnel, step, payload, completed_at, and all specified indexes.
subtasks.details: Include UNIQUE constraint on (user_id, org_id, funnel), CHECK constraint on funnel column values, and any other indexes per PRD §7.5. Use TIMESTAMPTZ for timestamps.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run migration forward and use \d onboarding_progress to verify columns, types, constraints, and indexes match PRD.

subtasks.title: Add kind column to orgs table
subtasks.description: ALTER TABLE orgs to add kind TEXT column with CHECK constraint limiting values to 'reseller' or 'customer'.
subtasks.details: Execute: ALTER TABLE orgs ADD COLUMN kind TEXT CHECK (kind IN ('reseller','customer')); Ensure constraint is named appropriately.
subtasks.testStrategy: After migration, verify column exists with \d orgs and test INSERT with invalid value to confirm CHECK fires.

subtasks.title: Add review_required and trial_ends_at to orgs
subtasks.description: ALTER TABLE orgs to add review_required BOOLEAN DEFAULT false and trial_ends_at TIMESTAMPTZ columns as per PRD §4.8.
subtasks.details: Execute: ALTER TABLE orgs ADD COLUMN review_required BOOLEAN DEFAULT false; ALTER TABLE orgs ADD COLUMN trial_ends_at TIMESTAMPTZ; Add to migration up function.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify columns, types, and default value with \d orgs; INSERT without values to check default behavior.

subtasks.title: Implement down migration and full testing
subtasks.description: Write DROP statements in down function for rollback; run complete forward/backward tests and verify all constraints/indexes.
subtasks.details: Down: DROP TABLE onboarding_progress; ALTER TABLE orgs DROP COLUMN trial_ends_at, DROP COLUMN review_required, DROP COLUMN kind. Test UNIQUE constraint by inserting duplicate (user_id, org_id, funnel).
subtasks.testStrategy: Run migration forward/backward multiple cycles. Use \d on both tables, insert test rows, confirm UNIQUE fires on duplicates, indexes exist via \di.

tasks.title: Migration: customer sub-org schema + invitation enhancements
tasks.description: Create migration 003_customer_suborg.sql adding orgs.parent_org_id, org_invitations.intended_role/team_id, and indexes as per PRD §5.8.
tasks.details:
[CROSS-TAG BLOCKER] Customer sub-org schema depends on reseller-tenancy #1 (parent_org_id, org type enum).

ALTER TABLE orgs ADD COLUMN parent_org_id UUID REFERENCES orgs(id) ON DELETE CASCADE; ALTER TABLE org_invitations ADD COLUMN intended_role TEXT, team_id UUID REFERENCES teams(id); CREATE INDEX on orgs(parent_org_id) WHERE parent_org_id IS NOT NULL; CREATE INDEX on org_invitations(intended_role);
tasks.testStrategy: Migration forward/backward. Verify foreign key constraints with cross-insert test. Confirm indexes with EXPLAIN ANALYZE on parent_org_id queries.

subtasks.title: Generate migration file 003_customer_suborg.sql
subtasks.description: Use npm run db:migrate:generate to create the empty migration file for customer sub-org schema changes.
subtasks.details: Run `npm run db:migrate:generate 003_customer_suborg` to scaffold the migration file with up/down functions in the correct migrations directory.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify file created with correct timestamp prefix and empty up/down functions using ls migrations/

subtasks.title: Implement orgs.parent_org_id column addition
subtasks.description: Add the parent_org_id column to orgs table with self-referencing foreign key and CASCADE delete.
subtasks.details: In up(): ALTER TABLE orgs ADD COLUMN parent_org_id UUID REFERENCES orgs(id) ON DELETE CASCADE; In down(): ALTER TABLE orgs DROP COLUMN parent_org_id;

subtasks.title: Implement org_invitations column additions
subtasks.description: Add intended_role and team_id columns to org_invitations table with foreign key constraint.
subtasks.details: In up(): ALTER TABLE org_invitations ADD COLUMN intended_role TEXT, ADD COLUMN team_id UUID REFERENCES teams(id); In down(): ALTER TABLE org_invitations DROP COLUMN team_id, DROP COLUMN intended_role;

subtasks.title: Create partial index on orgs.parent_org_id
subtasks.description: Add index on parent_org_id only for non-null values to optimize sub-org queries.
subtasks.details: In up(): CREATE INDEX ON orgs(parent_org_id) WHERE parent_org_id IS NOT NULL; In down(): DROP INDEX IF EXISTS orgs_parent_org_id_idx (use actual generated index name);
subtasks.testStrategy: After migration, run \di | grep parent_org_id to confirm index exists with WHERE clause.

subtasks.title: Create index on org_invitations.intended_role and test migration
subtasks.description: Add functional index on intended_role and validate complete migration forward/backward.
subtasks.details: In up(): CREATE INDEX ON org_invitations(intended_role); In down(): DROP INDEX IF EXISTS org_invitations_intended_role_idx; Test: npm run db:migrate:up 003 && npm run db:migrate:down 003
subtasks.testStrategy: Run migration forward/backward. Verify schema with \d orgs, \d org_invitations. Test FK: INSERT orgs(parent_org_id=nonexistent_uuid) fails. Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT * FROM orgs WHERE parent_org_id=? uses index.

tasks.title: Rebase invitation token hash-at-rest from upstream fix/hash-invitation-tokens
tasks.description: Integrate upstream branch ensuring tokens stored as SHA-256 hashes per PRD §7.1 and §8.4.
tasks.details:
[CROSS-TAG BLOCKER] Requires platform-ops #1 (upstream remote configured) before cherry-picking fix/hash-invitation-tokens.

Cherry-pick commits from feat/hash-invitation-tokens. Update InvitationService.createInvitation() to hash token immediately after nanoid(32) generation. Modify lookup to hash input and compare. Ensure plaintext returned once to email sender only.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test createInvitation() - verify DB contains hash not plaintext. Test acceptInvitation() with valid/invalid/expired tokens. Snapshot DB before/after to confirm no plaintext storage.

subtasks.title: Verify upstream branch and prepare local repo
subtasks.description: Check out the target branch, fetch latest changes from remote fix/hash-invitation-tokens, and verify authentication setup to prevent rebase failures.
subtasks.details: Run `git fetch origin`, confirm `git remote -v` shows correct URLs, check SSH key or update HTTPS token if needed. Stash any local changes with `git stash`. Ensure clean working directory.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify no authentication errors on fetch; confirm upstream branch exists with `git branch -r | grep fix/hash-invitation-tokens`.

subtasks.title: Cherry-pick commits from feat/hash-invitation-tokens
subtasks.description: Identify and cherry-pick relevant commits from feat/hash-invitation-tokens branch as specified in implementation details.
subtasks.details: Use `git log feat/hash-invitation-tokens --oneline` to list commits. Cherry-pick each with `git cherry-pick <commit-hash>`. Resolve any conflicts immediately by editing files and `git add`, then `git cherry-pick --continue`.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run `git log --oneline -10` to confirm cherry-picked commits appear with correct messages and no duplicates.

subtasks.title: Update InvitationService.createInvitation() for hashing
subtasks.description: Modify createInvitation() to generate nanoid(32), hash immediately with SHA-256, store hash in DB, and return plaintext only once for email sender.
subtasks.details: Import crypto library, generate token = nanoid(32), compute hash = crypto.createHash('sha256').update(token).digest('hex'), save hash to DB. Ensure plaintext is passed to email service but never stored or logged again.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: mock nanoid, call createInvitation(), assert DB contains hash not plaintext, verify email receives plaintext token.

subtasks.title: Modify invitation lookup and comparison logic
subtasks.description: Update lookup methods to hash input tokens with SHA-256 and compare against stored hashes for validation.
subtasks.details: In acceptInvitation() or lookup functions, take input token, compute hash = crypto.createHash('sha256').update(inputToken).digest('hex'), query DB for matching hash. Handle expired/used tokens by checking timestamps/status.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: valid token hashes match, invalid/expired don't match, case-sensitive comparison fails correctly.

subtasks.title: Rebase onto upstream, test, and force-push changes
subtasks.description: Rebase cherry-picked changes onto fix/hash-invitation-tokens, run tests, resolve conflicts, and force-push the updated branch.
subtasks.details: Run `git rebase origin/fix/hash-invitation-tokens`, resolve conflicts if any with `git add` and `git rebase --continue`. Run full test suite. Force-push with `git push origin <branch-name> --force-with-lease`.
subtasks.testStrategy: All unit/integration tests pass post-rebase (createInvitation hashes correctly, acceptInvitation validates, no plaintext in DB snapshots). CI pipeline green.

tasks.title: Server: OrgService.createCustomerSubOrg transaction
tasks.description: Implement atomic transaction in src/org/org-service.ts for Funnel B provisioning per PRD §5.4.
tasks.details: Add createCustomerSubOrg({resellerId, customer_name, primary_admin_email, plan_id, brand_override?, vendor_allowlist?, domain?}) returning new org_id. Steps: 1) INSERT orgs (kind='customer', parent_org_id=resellerId,...), 2) INSERT tool_allowlists seeded from reseller or override, 3) INSERT onboarding_progress(step='customer_admin_pending'), 4) If send_invite_now call InvitationService.createInvitation(intended_role='owner'). Use DB transaction with rollback.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit/integration tests: happy path creates all 4 resources; failure in step 3 rolls back all; email conflict prevents creation; verify tool_allowlists inherits correctly.

subtasks.title: Add createCustomerSubOrg method signature and transaction wrapper
subtasks.description: Implement the method signature in src/org/org-service.ts accepting all required parameters and wrap the entire logic in a database transaction with automatic rollback on failure.
subtasks.details: Use TypeScript function with params: resellerId (UUID), customer_name (string), primary_admin_email (string), plan_id (UUID), optional brand_override, vendor_allowlist, domain, send_invite_now (boolean). Employ DB transaction API (e.g., pg transaction or ORM equivalent) to ensure atomicity across all steps.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test method signature validation; verify transaction starts correctly and rolls back on early exception.

subtasks.title: Implement INSERT orgs for customer sub-organization
subtasks.description: Within the transaction, execute INSERT into orgs table creating a new customer org with kind='customer', parent_org_id=resellerId, and other fields like name, email, plan_id, domain.
subtasks.details: Capture the generated new org_id from INSERT result. Include fields: name=customer_name, primary_admin_email, plan_id, domain if provided, kind='customer', parent_org_id=resellerId. Handle any unique constraint on email.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify org created with correct parent_org_id and kind; test email uniqueness conflict rolls back transaction.

subtasks.title: Implement INSERT tool_allowlists seeded from reseller or override
subtasks.description: Query reseller's tool_allowlist, merge/override with vendor_allowlist param if provided, then INSERT into tool_allowlists linked to the new org_id.
subtasks.details: Fetch reseller allowlist via query on org_id=resellerId. If vendor_allowlist provided, use it; else inherit from reseller. INSERT records associating with new org_id, ensuring foreign key integrity within transaction.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test inheritance: no override copies reseller list; override uses provided list; failure in this step rolls back org insert.

subtasks.title: Implement INSERT onboarding_progress and conditional invitation
subtasks.description: INSERT into onboarding_progress with step='customer_admin_pending' for new org_id; if send_invite_now, call InvitationService.createInvitation with intended_role='owner' and primary_admin_email.
subtasks.details: onboarding_progress: org_id=new_org_id, step='customer_admin_pending'. For invitation: pass new_org_id, email=primary_admin_email, role='owner'. Ensure InvitationService call is within transaction or handles rollback.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify progress inserted; test send_invite_now true creates invitation, false skips it; simulate InvitationService failure for rollback.

subtasks.title: Finalize transaction commit and return new org_id
subtasks.description: Commit the transaction on success, return the new org_id; add error handling to rollback and propagate exceptions.
subtasks.details: After all inserts/invitation, commit transaction and return { org_id: new_org_id }. Catch any errors, ensure rollback, re-raise with context (e.g., 'Failed to create customer sub-org').
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test full happy path creates all 4 resources; inject failures at each step to confirm rollback (no partial state); verify return value.

tasks.title: Server: POST /orgs/:resellerId/customers route with guards
tasks.description: Add Fastify route in src/org/routes/org-crud.ts with role/rate-limit per PRD §5.2, §8.1, §8.2.
tasks.details: POST /orgs/:resellerId/customers - require org_members.role IN ('owner','admin') on reseller org. Rate limit 20/hour per reseller_id using @fastify/rate-limit key=resellerId. Validate primary_admin_email not conflicting. Call OrgService.createCustomerSubOrg. Emit audit customer_org.created.
tasks.testStrategy: Auth tests: owner success, admin success, member 403, unauth 401. Rate limit: 21st request 429. Integration: full form validation + transaction rollback simulation.

subtasks.title: Add POST /orgs/:resellerId/customers route skeleton
subtasks.description: Define the basic Fastify POST route structure in src/org/routes/org-crud.ts with proper URL pattern and empty handler.
subtasks.details: Use fastify.post('/orgs/:resellerId/customers', handler) following Fastify route declaration patterns. Ensure :resellerId param is properly captured for guards and service calls.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify route registration by checking Fastify print routes output includes the endpoint.

subtasks.title: Implement role-based access guard for reseller org
subtasks.description: Add preHandler to check if authenticated user has org_members.role IN ('owner','admin') for the reseller org specified by :resellerId.
subtasks.details: Query org_members table joining on orgs.id = :resellerId and user_id from session. Return 403 if not owner/admin, 401 if unauthenticated. Use existing auth decorators.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: owner success, admin success, member 403, unauth 401 using mocked DB/session.

subtasks.title: Configure rate limiting by resellerId
subtasks.description: Apply @fastify/rate-limit with 20/hour limit using key=resellerId extracted from route params.
subtasks.details: Register rate-limit plugin at route/plugin level with { keyGenerator: (req) => req.params.resellerId, max: 20, timeWindow: '1h' }. Handle 429 responses gracefully.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: 20 successful requests pass, 21st returns 429 with correct headers.

subtasks.title: Add request validation and email conflict check
subtasks.description: Define JSON schema for request body and validate primary_admin_email doesn't conflict with existing org_members/org_invitations.
subtasks.details: Schema: { customer_name: {type:'string'}, primary_admin_email: {type:'email'}, plan_id: {type:'string'}, ... }. Query for email conflicts across relevant tables before proceeding.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test valid/invalid payloads return 400. Email conflict returns specific 409 error message.

subtasks.title: Implement service call and audit logging
subtasks.description: Call OrgService.createCustomerSubOrg with validated params and emit 'customer_org.created' audit event on success.
subtasks.details: Pass {resellerId, customer_name, primary_admin_email, plan_id, ...} to service. On success: return 201 with new org_id/details, emit audit event with relevant metadata.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: full happy path creates sub-org and emits audit. Mock service failure returns 500.

tasks.title: Email: branded customer-admin-invite template
tasks.description: Create src/web/templates/emails/customer-admin-invite.ts per PRD §5.5 using reseller brand.
tasks.details: Template takes brand: BrandConfig, customer_name, reseller_name, invite_url. Use reseller subdomain if verified else default. Add List-Unsubscribe header. Plain-text alternate. Integrate with existing transactional email pipeline from src/org/routes/invitations.ts.
tasks.testStrategy: Render test with mock brand - verify logo/color/from-address. Send test email to self, confirm delivery/branding. Test fallback to platform default brand.

subtasks.title: Create branded HTML email template structure
subtasks.description: Build the main HTML structure for customer-admin-invite email in src/web/templates/emails/customer-admin-invite.ts using reseller BrandConfig with logo, colors, and from-address.
subtasks.details: Include dynamic placeholders for customer_name, reseller_name, invite_url. Use responsive design compatible with major email clients. Reference PRD §5.5 for content structure and best practices from invitation email templates.
subtasks.testStrategy: Render test with mock BrandConfig - verify logo displays, colors applied, from-address matches brand.email.

subtasks.title: Implement reseller brand resolution logic
subtasks.description: Add logic to resolve reseller brand: use verified subdomain brand if available, otherwise fallback to platform default brand.
subtasks.details: Integrate with src/brand/index.ts resolution. Check reseller domain verification status. Handle cases where brand data is missing or incomplete.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: verified reseller shows custom brand; unverified falls back to default; missing brand uses platform default.

subtasks.title: Add plain-text alternate and List-Unsubscribe header
subtasks.description: Create plain-text version of email and add required List-Unsubscribe header for compliance.
subtasks.details: Generate plain-text from HTML content. Construct List-Unsubscribe header with unsubscribe URL based on invite_url. Ensure multipart MIME structure.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate MIME structure parses correctly; confirm List-Unsubscribe header present and formatted per RFC.

subtasks.title: Define TypeScript interface and export template function
subtasks.description: Create typed interface for template inputs and export render function taking brand, customer_name, reseller_name, invite_url.
subtasks.details: Interface: { brand: BrandConfig, customer_name: string, reseller_name: string, invite_url: string }. Return { html: string, text: string, headers: Record<string,string> }.
subtasks.testStrategy: TypeScript compile test; runtime render with all params produces valid output object.

subtasks.title: Integrate template with invitations.ts pipeline
subtasks.description: Update src/org/routes/invitations.ts to use new customer-admin-invite template when generating emails.
subtasks.details: In InvitationService.createInvitation(), detect customer admin invites and call new template renderer. Pass resolved brand from org context. Ensure existing email pipeline unchanged for other invite types.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: trigger customer admin invite → verify correct template rendered with proper branding → send test email to self and confirm delivery/appearance.

tasks.title: UI: reseller Add Customer form + confirmation
tasks.description: Build /admin/customers/new form per PRD §5.3 table with server-rendered preview.
tasks.details: Server-rendered form in src/web/templates/onboarding-customer.ts style. Fields per PRD table. Multi-select vendor_allowlist from reseller's allowlist. Live brand preview. POST to /orgs/:resellerId/customers. Success: confirmation screen with customer details + admin invite status.
tasks.testStrategy: Form validation: required fields, email conflict error. Brand inheritance test. E2E: submit → 201 → confirmation renders correctly.

subtasks.title: Create server-rendered Add Customer form template
subtasks.description: Build the base form template at /admin/customers/new following src/web/templates/onboarding-customer.ts style with all fields from PRD §5.3 table.
subtasks.details: Include form fields per PRD table: customer name, primary_admin_email, vendor_allowlist multi-select. Use reseller context from route params. Add CSRF protection and POST action to /orgs/:resellerId/customers.
subtasks.testStrategy: Render test: verify all form fields present and styled correctly. Check reseller-specific vendor options load from DB.

subtasks.title: Implement multi-select vendor_allowlist dropdown
subtasks.description: Add multi-select component for vendor_allowlist populated from the reseller's allowlist vendors.
subtasks.details: Query reseller's vendor_allowlist from DB. Use searchable multi-select UI component. Pre-populate with reseller's current vendors. Include validation for at least one vendor selected.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: vendor list fetches correctly for reseller. UI test: select/deselect vendors updates form state correctly.

subtasks.title: Add live brand preview functionality
subtasks.description: Implement real-time brand preview that updates as form fields change, inheriting from reseller brand.
subtasks.details: Create preview pane showing customer branding with reseller logo/colors/fonts. Update on customer_name, vendor selection changes. Use server-side preview generation or client-side preview with form state.
subtasks.testStrategy: E2E test: change form fields → verify preview updates instantly with correct brand inheritance.

subtasks.title: Build form validation and error handling
subtasks.description: Add client/server validation for required fields and email conflicts with existing customers.
subtasks.details: Client-side: required fields, valid email format. Server-side: check primary_admin_email uniqueness, vendor_allowlist not empty. Display inline errors and global error banner for conflicts.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test invalid email → error message. Test duplicate email → server conflict error displays. Test missing required fields → prevents submission.

subtasks.title: Create success confirmation screen
subtasks.description: Build confirmation page showing created customer details and admin invite status after successful POST.
subtasks.details: Redirect to /admin/customers/:customerId/confirmation on 201. Display customer name, email, vendors, brand preview. Show invite status (sent/pending). Include back to customers list link.
subtasks.testStrategy: E2E: complete form submission → verify 201 response → confirmation renders with correct customer details and invite status.

tasks.title: Server: public /signup route with Auth0 + rate limits
tasks.description: Implement src/auth/signup.ts and Fastify plugin src/onboarding/reseller-signup.ts per PRD §4.3.
tasks.details: GET/POST /signup public, rate-limit 10/min IP + 3/min email domain. Three auth paths via Auth0 (email/password, Google, Microsoft). Form: email, password(if needed), organization_name, expected_customer_count, agreed_to_terms. Create Auth0 user → orgs(kind='reseller') → org_members(role='owner') in transaction. Set session, redirect /onboarding/reseller/welcome.
tasks.testStrategy: Rate limit tests. Auth paths: all three create correct records. Transaction rollback on Auth0 failure. Verify email_verified gate bypassed for federated.

subtasks.title: Implement rate limiting for /signup route
subtasks.description: Set up Fastify rate limiters for IP (10/min) and email domain (3/min) on GET/POST /signup endpoints.
subtasks.details: Use @fastify/rate-limit with keyFunction for 'ip' and custom 'email_domain' extraction from body. Configure max=10, timeWindow=60*1000 for IP; max=3 for domain. Apply to both GET and POST handlers before other middleware.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test 11th IP request returns 429; 4th domain request 429. Verify different IPs/domains allowed independently.

subtasks.title: Create GET /signup handler and form schema
subtasks.description: Implement public GET /signup returning signup form with fields: email, password (conditional), organization_name, expected_customer_count, agreed_to_terms.
subtasks.details: In src/auth/signup.ts, define Fastify GET route (no auth). Use Zod schema for form validation. Render server-side template with conditional password field based on auth method selection (email/password, Google, Microsoft).
subtasks.testStrategy: GET returns 200 with form HTML; schema validates all field types/constraints; no auth required.

subtasks.title: Implement POST /signup with Auth0 authentication paths
subtasks.description: Handle POST /signup form submission for three Auth0 paths: email/password, Google OAuth, Microsoft OAuth.
subtasks.details: In src/auth/signup.ts POST handler: validate form with Zod. Route to Auth0: 1) email/password → Auth0 Database signup; 2) Google/Microsoft → federated OAuth flow. Extract user_id/email from Auth0 callback. Handle rate limits from subtask 1.
subtasks.testStrategy: Mock Auth0 responses: each path succeeds, returns user profile; invalid creds → 400; rate limit triggers 429.

subtasks.title: Implement transactional DB creation for org and membership
subtasks.description: Create Auth0 user → INSERT orgs(kind='reseller') → INSERT org_members(role='owner') all in single DB transaction.
subtasks.details: After Auth0 success, use DB transaction: 1) INSERT orgs (kind='reseller', name=organization_name, expected_customer_count); 2) INSERT org_members (org_id, user_id from Auth0, role='owner'); Rollback on any failure. Use values from form.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration: full flow creates 3 records or none (rollback); Auth0 failure → no DB changes; duplicate email → Auth0 error, no DB writes.

subtasks.title: Add Fastify plugin, session setup, and redirect
subtasks.description: Register signup routes as Fastify plugin in src/onboarding/reseller-signup.ts, set user session, redirect to /onboarding/reseller/welcome.
subtasks.details: Export Fastify plugin registering both GET/POST /signup from src/auth/signup.ts. On success: set fastify session with user/org data, permanent redirect (308) to /onboarding/reseller/welcome. Ensure public route (no auth guard).
subtasks.testStrategy: E2E: form submit → Auth0 → DB tx → session set → 308 redirect; verify session contains correct org_id/user_id.

tasks.title: Billing: resellerPlans catalog + Stripe checkout

tasks.details: Add resellerPlans: Launch/Growth/Scale with Stripe product/price IDs, 14-day trial. Update checkout.ts for reseller plan SKUs. Webhook success: set orgs.trial_ends_at=NOW()+14d. Require email_verified=true before billing screen.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit: plan catalog lookup. Integration: mock Stripe checkout → webhook → trial_ends_at set. Test email_verified gate blocks unverified users.

subtasks.title: Add resellerPlans to plan-catalog.ts
subtasks.description: Extend src/billing/plan-catalog.ts to include Launch/Growth/Scale reseller tiers with corresponding Stripe product and price IDs per PRD §4.5.
subtasks.details: Define resellerPlans array with plan objects containing name, Stripe product ID, price ID, and 14-day trial configuration. Ensure compatibility with existing plan lookup functions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: verify resellerPlans returns correct tiers and Stripe IDs for each plan type.

subtasks.title: Update checkout.ts for reseller plan SKUs
subtasks.description: Modify src/billing/checkout.ts to handle reseller plan selection using the new SKUs from resellerPlans.
subtasks.details: Add logic to accept reseller plan identifiers, map to Stripe price IDs, create Checkout Sessions with trial_period_days=14, and validate org context.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: mock plan lookup and verify Checkout Session creation with correct price ID and trial period.

subtasks.title: Implement email_verified guard in billing flow
subtasks.description: Add middleware or guard in billing screen/route to require user.email_verified=true before allowing access to checkout.
subtasks.details: Check user.email_verified in route handler or before rendering billing page; redirect unverified users to verification flow with clear error message.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: unverified user accessing billing redirects; verified user proceeds to checkout.

subtasks.title: Handle Stripe webhook for reseller trial activation
subtasks.description: Update Stripe webhook handler for checkout.session.completed to set orgs.trial_ends_at = NOW() + 14 days on success.
subtasks.details: In webhook success path, query org by Stripe customer/session metadata, update trial_ends_at using database transaction, emit audit event.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: mock Stripe webhook → verify trial_ends_at updated correctly in database.

subtasks.title: Add comprehensive testing for reseller billing flow
subtasks.description: Implement unit and integration tests covering plan catalog, checkout, guards, and webhook per task test strategy.
subtasks.details: Unit: plan catalog lookup. Integration: full flow with mock Stripe (checkout → webhook → trial set). E2E: email_verified blocks unverified users.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run full test suite: verify 100% coverage for new reseller billing paths, including edge cases like invalid SKUs.

tasks.title: Wizard framework + reseller onboarding tour
tasks.description: Build src/web/wizard.ts framework and 3-step reseller tour per PRD §4.6, §7.2.
tasks.details: WizardStep type exactly as PRD code sample. Steps: 1) customer survey → onboarding_progress.payload, 2) vendor allowlist selection → tool_allowlists, 3) brand setup + subdomain queue. Persist progress in onboarding_progress(funnel='reseller'). Server-rendered, form-POST/step. Skippable with analytics.
tasks.testStrategy: Resume test: complete step1, close tab, verify jumps to step2. Skip test emits analytics. Completion updates onboarding_progress.completed_at.

subtasks.title: Define WizardStep type and core wizard framework
subtasks.description: Create the WizardStep type exactly matching PRD code sample and implement the base wizard framework in src/web/wizard.ts supporting server-rendered steps with form-POST navigation.
subtasks.details: Implement WizardStep interface with required properties per PRD. Build framework handling step progression, persistence to onboarding_progress table (funnel='reseller'), and skippable steps with analytics events.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test WizardStep type validation. Verify framework initializes correctly and handles basic step navigation.

subtasks.title: Implement Step 1: Customer survey form
subtasks.description: Build the first wizard step collecting customer survey data, persisting to onboarding_progress.payload via form POST.
subtasks.details: Create server-rendered form for survey inputs as specified in PRD §4.6. On submit, update onboarding_progress.payload JSON field. Add skip button emitting analytics event.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test form submission updates payload correctly. Resume test: complete step1, close tab, verify jumps to step2 on reload.

subtasks.title: Implement Step 2: Vendor allowlist selection
subtasks.description: Build second wizard step for reseller to select vendor allowlist, saving to tool_allowlists table.
subtasks.details: Server-rendered multi-select form pulling from reseller's available tools. POST updates tool_allowlists with selected vendors. Support skip with analytics and progress persistence.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify selections save to tool_allowlists. Test skip emits analytics event without saving.

subtasks.title: Implement Step 3: Brand setup and subdomain queue
subtasks.description: Create final wizard step for brand configuration and subdomain queue submission per PRD §7.2.
subtasks.details: Server-rendered form for brand settings with live preview. On completion, queue subdomain, mark onboarding_progress.completed_at, and show success screen. Fully skippable.
subtasks.testStrategy: E2E test: complete all steps → verify completed_at timestamp set and subdomain queued.

subtasks.title: Add skip functionality, analytics, and resume logic
subtasks.description: Integrate skippable behavior across all steps with analytics tracking and progress resumption from onboarding_progress.
subtasks.details: Implement global skip handler emitting analytics events. Add resume logic checking onboarding_progress for current step. Ensure server-side state consistency on page reloads.
subtasks.testStrategy: Skip test: skip all steps → verify analytics emitted and funnel marked complete. Resume test across browser sessions.

tasks.title: UI: first-customer inline launch + dashboard banner
tasks.description: Implement Funnel A→B bridge per PRD §4.7 with dismissible banner.
tasks.details: End of reseller wizard: inline launch /admin/customers/new. Dashboard: query onboarding_progress WHERE funnel='reseller' AND step!='activated' → show banner until dismissed or customer added. Store banner dismissal in payload JSONB.
tasks.testStrategy: E2E: skip first-customer → banner shows → dismiss → banner gone. Add customer → banner gone. Refresh persists state.

subtasks.title: Implement reseller wizard end inline launch to /admin/customers/new
subtasks.description: At the end of the reseller wizard, add inline launch functionality that navigates or embeds the /admin/customers/new page to guide first customer creation.
subtasks.details: Modify wizard completion handler to render /admin/customers/new inline instead of redirect. Ensure seamless transition with shared state from wizard session. Handle responsive layout for embedded customer form.
subtasks.testStrategy: E2E: Complete reseller wizard → verify inline customer form appears without page reload → form pre-populates org context.

subtasks.title: Create dashboard banner query for reseller funnel progress
subtasks.description: Implement backend query to check onboarding_progress for funnel='reseller' AND step!='activated' to determine if banner should display.
subtasks.details: Write SQL query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM onboarding_progress WHERE funnel='reseller' AND step!='activated'. Cache result in session or Redis for dashboard loads. Return boolean flag to frontend.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit: Test query with mock data → returns true when step pending, false when activated. Integration: Verify query executes <50ms with index.

subtasks.title: Build dismissible dashboard banner UI component
subtasks.description: Create React/Vue component for dashboard banner that shows only when query condition met, with dismiss button storing state in payload JSONB.
subtasks.details: Design banner with call-to-action linking to /admin/customers/new. Add dismiss handler → POST /api/banner-dismiss with org_id, set payload.dismissed_at timestamp. Style as non-intrusive top banner.
subtasks.testStrategy: Component: Render with show=true/false → verify visibility. Click dismiss → API call with correct payload → banner hides.

subtasks.title: Add backend endpoint to store and check banner dismissal state
subtasks.description: Create API endpoint to store banner dismissal in org payload JSONB and update query logic to respect dismissed state.
subtasks.details: POST /api/banner-dismiss: UPDATE orgs SET payload = payload || jsonb_build_object('banner_dismissed_at', NOW()) WHERE id=$1. Modify banner query: AND (payload->>'banner_dismissed_at' IS NULL OR payload->>'banner_dismissed_at' < NOW() - interval '30 days').
subtasks.testStrategy: API: POST dismiss → verify JSONB updated. Query after dismiss → returns false. Add customer → query false independent of dismiss.

subtasks.title: Implement E2E tests for banner lifecycle and customer addition flow
subtasks.description: Write comprehensive E2E tests covering banner display, dismissal persistence, customer addition hiding banner, and state persistence on refresh.
subtasks.details: Test scenarios: 1) Fresh reseller → banner shows → dismiss → refresh → gone. 2) Banner shows → add customer → banner gone. 3) Dismiss + add customer → both conditions clear banner. Use Playwright/Cypress.
subtasks.testStrategy: E2E coverage: 100% pass rate across browsers. Verify DB state post-test matches expectations. Test edge: partial onboarding_progress records.

tasks.title: Subdomain verification job + brand setup
tasks.description: Async job for custom subdomain verification per PRD §4.6, §7.4.
tasks.details: New reseller_subdomains table (add to migration 002). Queue job on slug selection. Publish CNAME target in UI. Poll DNS until verified. Fallback to conduit.wyre.tld until success. Dashboard shows status + recheck button.
tasks.testStrategy: Mock DNS job: pending→verified→active. Email template test: verified subdomain in From vs fallback.

subtasks.title: Create reseller_subdomains database table
subtasks.description: Add new reseller_subdomains table to existing migration 002 with required columns for subdomain verification tracking.
subtasks.details: Include columns: reseller_id (UUID FK to orgs), slug (TEXT unique), cname_target (TEXT), status (ENUM: pending, verified, active, failed), verified_at (TIMESTAMP), created_at/updated_at. Add indexes on reseller_id and slug.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run migration forward/backward. Verify table schema with \d reseller_subdomains. Test FK constraint by inserting invalid reseller_id.

subtasks.title: Implement subdomain verification queue job
subtasks.description: Create async queue job that polls DNS for CNAME record matching the published target until verified or timeout.
subtasks.details: Job logic: Query DNS for slug.reseller_domain CNAME == cname_target. Update status to 'verified'/'active' on success. Implement exponential backoff polling (every 5min up to 24hrs). Log failures. Fallback routing to conduit.wyre.tld until active.
subtasks.testStrategy: Mock DNS resolver returning pending→verified→active states. Verify job transitions status correctly. Test fallback routing with unverified status.

subtasks.title: Add slug selection and job queueing to customer creation
subtasks.description: Queue verification job immediately when reseller selects custom subdomain slug during customer creation.
subtasks.details: In /orgs/:resellerId/customers POST handler: Validate slug uniqueness, insert to reseller_subdomains with status='pending', dispatch verification job with job_id. Return 202 Accepted with polling endpoint.
subtasks.testStrategy: Submit form with slug → verify DB insert + job queued. Check job_id matches reseller_subdomains record. Test duplicate slug → 409 Conflict.

subtasks.title: Build dashboard UI for subdomain status display
subtasks.description: Add subdomain status display + manual recheck button to reseller admin dashboard.
subtasks.details: Server-rendered table in dashboard: slug, status badge (pending/yellow, verified/green, failed/red), verified_at, actions (recheck button → POST /admin/subdomains/:id/reverify → queue job). Live status polling via SSE/WebSocket.
subtasks.testStrategy: Render dashboard with mock records (pending/verified/failed). Click recheck → verify job re-queued. E2E: status updates in real-time via SSE.

subtasks.title: Integrate brand setup with verification status
subtasks.description: Wire brand configuration to use verified subdomain in UI/email templates with fallback to conduit.wyre.tld.
subtasks.details: Update BrandConfig resolver: if reseller_subdomains.status='active' then from_domain=slug.reseller_domain else 'conduit.wyre.tld'. Publish CNAME target (e.g., 'cdn.wyre-platform.com') in customer creation UI. Update all brand-dependent components (emails, headers).
subtasks.testStrategy: Mock verification states → verify BrandConfig returns correct from_domain/CNAME. Test email template render with verified vs fallback. Confirm UI displays correct CNAME target.

tasks.title: Funnel C: branded invite page + welcome interstitial
tasks.description: Extend src/org/routes/invitations.ts for target org branding per PRD §6.1, §6.5.
tasks.details: renderInvitePage(): resolve brand from target org (reseller→customer inheritance). Post-accept: render welcome interstitial before /dashboard. Use src/brand/index.ts resolution.
tasks.testStrategy: GET /invite/<token>: verify customer sub-org shows reseller brand not Wyre. Expired/unknown token: same generic error page. Post-accept flow renders interstitial.

subtasks.title: Implement brand resolution for target org in renderInvitePage
subtasks.description: Modify renderInvitePage() in src/org/routes/invitations.ts to resolve brand from target organization using reseller-to-customer inheritance logic.
subtasks.details: Integrate src/brand/index.ts resolution function. Handle cases: direct customer org brand, inherit from reseller if customer lacks brand, fallback to platform default. Extract target org from invite token.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: mock invite token → verify returns reseller brand for customer sub-org, customer brand for direct org, default for no brand.

subtasks.title: Update invite page rendering to apply resolved brand
subtasks.description: Extend renderInvitePage() to render the invite page UI using the resolved brand configuration from target org.
subtasks.details: Pass resolved BrandConfig to template/renderer. Apply brand-specific styles, logo, colors, fonts per PRD §6.1. Ensure responsive design and accessibility.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: GET /invite/<token> → verify HTML includes reseller logo/colors for customer sub-org, not Wyre default.

subtasks.title: Create welcome interstitial component or template
subtasks.description: Design and implement a new welcome interstitial page/template that displays branded welcome message post-invite acceptance.
subtasks.details: Use same brand resolution as invite page (src/brand/index.ts). Include elements like welcome message, next steps, org info per PRD §6.5. Make dismissible with auto-redirect to /dashboard.
subtasks.testStrategy: Render test: mock brand → verify displays correct logo, colors, content. Test auto-redirect timer and manual dismiss button.

subtasks.title: Modify post-accept flow to render welcome interstitial
subtasks.description: Update invite acceptance handler in invitations.ts to render welcome interstitial before redirecting to /dashboard.
subtasks.details: After successful invite acceptance (user creation/org join), intercept /dashboard redirect. Render interstitial with resolved brand, store dismiss state in session/localStorage to prevent re-showing.
subtasks.testStrategy: E2E test: accept invite → verify interstitial shows before dashboard, dismiss → lands on dashboard, refresh dashboard → no interstitial.

subtasks.title: Add comprehensive tests for branded invite flow and error handling
subtasks.description: Implement full test suite covering branded rendering, inheritance, interstitial flow, and error cases as specified in task test strategy.
subtasks.details: Cover: GET /invite/valid_token (customer shows reseller brand), expired/unknown token (generic error page), post-accept interstitial. Use mocks for brand resolution and DB.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run full suite: verify 100% pass rate. Check console/network for no errors. Test edge cases: no org brand, broken inheritance, session expiry.

tasks.title: Funnel C: Auth0 connection hint for domain federation
tasks.description: Add src/auth/auth0.ts support for connection hint per PRD §6.3.
tasks.details: In /auth/login?invite=<token>, check invite→org.domain vs user email domain + reseller domain_trust_enabled → append connection hint to /authorize URL. Default to Universal Login otherwise.
tasks.testStrategy: Test domain match → correct connection pre-selected. No match → default login. Verify state param carries invite token across.

subtasks.title: Add invite token parsing in /auth/login route
subtasks.description: Extend the /auth/login route handler to extract and validate the invite token from query parameters.
subtasks.details: In the route handler for /auth/login?invite=<token>, parse the invite parameter, validate it exists and is a valid token format, then fetch the corresponding invite record from the database to retrieve org details.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test with valid/invalid/missing invite token: valid fetches org data, invalid returns error, missing defaults to standard login flow.

subtasks.title: Implement domain matching logic for invite org
subtasks.description: Create a function to compare invite organization domain against user email domain and reseller settings.
subtasks.details: Write a helper function that takes invite org.domain, extracts domain from user email (or detects from form input), checks reseller.domain_trust_enabled flag, and determines if domain federation applies.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: matching domains return true, non-matching false, reseller flag off disables match, various email formats parsed correctly.

subtasks.title: Update auth0.ts to conditionally append connection hint
subtasks.description: Modify src/auth/auth0.ts to build /authorize URL with connection hint parameter when domain match succeeds.
subtasks.details: In the Auth0 authorize URL generation, add logic: if domainMatch && reseller.domain_trust_enabled, append ?connection=<org_domain_or_connection_id> to target federated connection; else use default Universal Login.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify generated URLs: domain match includes connection hint, no match omits it and uses universal login params.

subtasks.title: Ensure state parameter preserves invite token
subtasks.description: Modify Auth0 state parameter to encode and carry the original invite token through the authentication flow.
subtasks.details: Base64 encode invite token and include in Auth0 state param (state=invite:<encoded_token>|other_state); on callback, decode and validate state carries valid invite token before redirecting.
subtasks.testStrategy: Full flow test: login with invite → state roundtrip preserves token → post-auth callback extracts and validates invite token correctly.

subtasks.title: Add comprehensive tests for connection hint logic
subtasks.description: Implement end-to-end tests covering domain matching, connection selection, and fallback behaviors.
subtasks.details: Write integration tests simulating: domain match → federated connection pre-selected; no match → Universal Login; invalid/expired invite → generic error; verify state param integrity across full Auth0 flow.
subtasks.testStrategy: E2E tests with mocked Auth0 responses: correct connection pre-selection on match, default login on mismatch, state param carries invite token, error handling for edge cases.

tasks.title: Customer admin tour templates

tasks.details: 3 steps: 1) Welcome showing provisioned plan/vendors, 2) Team invite form calling InvitationService, 3) Vendor connect picker filtered by allowlist. Update onboarding_progress.step='activated' on completion. Emit customer.activated.
tasks.testStrategy: Render each step with mock data. E2E: complete tour → step='activated' + analytics event.

subtasks.title: Create base onboarding-customer.ts template structure

subtasks.testStrategy: Unit: verify steps array length=3, navigation doesn't crash on bounds.

subtasks.title: Implement Step 1: Welcome screen with plan/vendors

subtasks.testStrategy: Render with mock customer data - verify plan name and vendor count display correctly.

subtasks.testStrategy: Render with mock allowlist - verify only allowed vendors shown, selection state persists.

subtasks.description: Implement finish handler updating onboarding_progress.step='activated' and emitting customer.activated event.

subtasks.testStrategy: E2E: complete all steps → verify DB update to 'activated', event emitted, analytics logged.

tasks.title: Fraud heuristics + review_required guard
tasks.description: Async job post-signup checking fraud signals, blocks addCustomer if review_required=true per PRD §4.4.
tasks.details: Post-signup job: check burner domains, bad IP ranges, velocity → SET orgs.review_required=true. Guard in createCustomerSubOrg(): if parent.review_required then 403.
tasks.testStrategy: Mock fraud signals → review_required=true → addCustomer 403. Wyre admin clears → success.

subtasks.title: Implement post-signup async fraud check job
subtasks.description: Create an asynchronous job that triggers after user signup to evaluate fraud signals including burner domains, bad IP ranges, and velocity checks.
subtasks.details: Use a queue system like Bull or Agenda.js to process jobs. Fetch signup data (email domain, IP, timestamps). Implement checks: burner domain lists (e.g., disposable-email-domains), IP blocklists (e.g., IP2Proxy), velocity (e.g., signups per IP/hour > threshold).
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: mock signup data with burner/bad IP/high velocity → job completes without error. Integration: trigger job → queue processes correctly.

subtasks.title: Develop fraud signal detection functions
subtasks.description: Build specific functions to detect burner email domains, bad IP ranges, and signup velocity anomalies.
subtasks.details: Create isBurnerDomain(email), isBadIP(ip), checkVelocity(ip, window_hours). Use external lists/APIs for domains/IPs (cache in Redis). Velocity: Redis counters with TTL. Return fraud score or boolean flags.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: valid burner domains/IPs return true; edge cases like subdomains, IPv6. Velocity: increment counters → threshold breach detected.

subtasks.title: Add review_required flag update logic
subtasks.description: If any fraud signal is detected, update the organization's review_required field to true in the database.
subtasks.details: In job handler: if isBurner || isBadIP || velocityBreach → UPDATE orgs SET review_required=true WHERE org_id = signup_org_id. Use transaction. Log fraud reasons for audit.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests: fraud signals trigger → DB row updated to true. No signals → remains false. Concurrent jobs don't race.

subtasks.title: Implement review_required guard in createCustomerSubOrg
subtasks.description: Add guard in OrgService.createCustomerSubOrg to block creation if parent org has review_required=true, returning 403.
subtasks.details: In src/org/org-service.ts: before transaction, SELECT review_required FROM orgs WHERE id=resellerId. If true → throw ForbiddenError('Parent org requires review'). Integrate with existing Task 4 transaction logic.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: parent review_required=true → 403 error. False → proceeds to create. Mock DB query.

subtasks.title: Add comprehensive tests and documentation
subtasks.description: Write tests for full fraud job flow and guard; document heuristics, thresholds, and Wyre admin clearance process.
subtasks.details: E2E: signup with fraud signals → job sets flag → addCustomer 403. Clearance test: admin sets false → success. Doc: README with signal details, config vars for thresholds/lists.
subtasks.testStrategy: Mock entire flow: fraud → flag → block → manual clear → success. Coverage >90%. Load test: 100 concurrent signups.
